texto: The crux of my argument is this—we cannot truly understand what we should put on, say, or do, without looking at our behavior in relationship to Christ. In Christ, decency in behavior and dress is far larger than the particulars of modesty. By the world’s standards, modesty is a rule passed down from generation to generation which involves choosing a garment that submits to a certain standard set by a mother or community, one that constantly changes.” – Stacie Parlee-Johnson (Ch. 9, A Theology of Modesty)
explicacao: Orientação direta de Rosalie De Rosset sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “The crux of my argument is this—we cannot truly understand what we should put on, say, or do, wit…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, christ-s-righteousness, decency, útil em momentos de mudanca e decency.

texto: The goal of using our voice is not just to be noticed, but with humility to gracefully be fully present in relationships. Deferring to another person does not require that we be ignored; allowing that is cowardice. Hiding, a form of dishonesty, prevents true community.
explicacao: Citação de Rosalie De Rosset sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “The goal of using our voice is not just to be noticed, but with humility to gracefully be fully p…” articula fe, community, faith em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como faith e relationships.

texto: …what have you accumulated from the past—what are you in the process of accumulating that will be passed on, if not deliberately, then accidentally? Is this accumulation the best of what has been and the best of what is currently being written, sung, and created? Is it wise? And if not, what will be the next generation’s inheritance, your children’s legacy?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Rosalie De Rosset que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “…what have you accumulated from the past—what are you in the process of accumulating that will be…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, deliberation, discernment, com foco em discernment e future-generations.

texto: Young children begin very early to internalize information that either encourages or discourages self-disclosure. Cues are intuitively understood. Most of what we feel is unexamined and articulated. Cultural norms are unwittingly absorbed. We learn when to speak and when to stay silent. - Pam MacRae (Ch. 2)
explicacao: Citação de Rosalie De Rosset sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Young children begin very early to internalize information that either encourages or discourages …” articula sabedoria, childhood, children em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como estudos e children.
